info.name: LEAVE MANAGEMENT
info.description:
### Welcome to Postman! This is your first collection. 

Collections are your starting point for building and testing APIs. You can use this one to:

• Group related requests
• Test your API in real-world scenarios
• Document and share your requests

Update the name and overview whenever you’re ready to make it yours.

[Learn more about Postman Collections.](https://learning.postman.com/docs/collections/collections-overview/)

request.description:
Retrieves the leave balance for the authenticated employee for the current year.

**Authentication:** Requires a valid Bearer token via the `accessToken` variable.

**Response**

Returns a summary of leave balances broken down by leave type for the specified year.

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `year` | number | The year for which balances are returned |
| `balances` | array | List of leave balance objects |
| `balances[].leaveType` | string | Type of leave (e.g. `CASUAL`, `SICK`, `PRIVILEGE`) |
| `balances[].allocated` | number | Total days allocated for the year |
| `balances[].carriedForward` | number | Days carried forward from the previous year |
| `balances[].used` | number | Days already used |
| `balances[].remaining` | number | Days remaining |

**Example Response**
```json
{
  "year": 2026,
  "balances": [
    {
      "leaveType": "CASUAL",
      "allocated": 12,
      "carriedForward": 0,
      "used": 2,
      "remaining": 10
    },
    {
      "leaveType": "PRIVILEGE",
      "allocated": 15,
      "carriedForward": 0,
      "used": 0,
      "remaining": 15
    },
    {
      "leaveType": "SICK",
      "allocated": 10,
      "carriedForward": 0,
      "used": 0,
      "remaining": 10
    }
  ]
}
```

response.name: only pending leaves eligible for rejection
rejecting other team's employee leave
cannot cancel others leaves
only pending leaves can be cancelled
approving other team's employee leave
no more pending leaves

A successful GET response will have a `200 OK` status, and should include some kind of response body - for example, HTML web content or JSON data.
